Navigation (Hidden in Print) | Home | About | Contact

@media print CSS Test Page

This yellow box should ONLY appear on screen, NOT in the PDF!
This green box should ONLY appear in the PDF, NOT on screen!

Purpose

This page tests the HTML to PDF converter's ability to respect @media print CSS rules. The print version should have different styling than what you see in a web browser.

What's Different in Print?

  • Navigation bar is hidden
  • Yellow "screen only" box is hidden
  • Green "print only" box appears
  • Links show their URLs in parentheses
  • Colors are optimized for printing
  • Shadows and backgrounds are removed or simplified
  • Page breaks are controlled for better layout

Example Link

Visit the any2pdf repository for more information. In the PDF, the URL should appear after the link text.

Content Preservation

While styling changes for print, all essential content should be preserved. This includes:

  • All text content
  • Proper heading hierarchy
  • List formatting
  • Paragraph spacing
  • Table data (if present)

Page 2 Content

The content above this heading forces a page break in print mode. This tests the converter's ability to handle CSS page-break properties.

Print Optimization Features

Feature Screen Print
Background Colors Visible Optimized
Navigation Shown Hidden
Links Colored URLs shown
Page Breaks N/A Controlled

Success Criteria

If the PDF output shows:

  1. The green "PRINT VERSION" box (not the yellow screen one)
  2. No navigation bar at the top
  3. URLs after link text
  4. Proper page break before "Page 2 Content"
  5. Clean, print-friendly formatting

...then the @media print CSS support is working correctly!

Footer content - Styled differently in print mode

Generated by any2pdf HTML converter test suite
